verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a type I single-pass TM glycoprotein whose dominant steady-state localization is the trans-Golgi network. However, it is well-documented to constitutively cycle between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes (Ponnambalam et al. 1996; Reaves et al. 1993). A minority surface pool exists at steady state through its own TM anchor. This fits dual_localization: the protein has its own TM-based anchor at the PM during the surface stage, but the dominant compartment is endomembrane (TGN/endosomes), not the plasma membrane.
